文尼多謝利迷支持︰好多謝各位利迷為我做既一切，當我(對熱刺)被換出時聽到球迷既支持、讚賞對我黎講意義重大，對我好大鼓舞，我明白有時隊波唔順做球迷都好辛苦，但利迷就係會時時刻刻一直係背後支持球隊及推動大家向前，所以我地都會盡最大努力以行動及表現報答大家。
2017年至今成績麻麻，對每個人都唔好受，即使我身在非洲參加非國盃，當唔使比賽或操練時我都會睇利記既比賽為隊友打氣，人雖然唔係度，但我既心一直都係利記，依家球隊反彈，我地希望一直保持好表現，雖然最後有咩結果仍然言之尚早，但球隊信心已經番晒黎，目標係踢好下一場比賽，並一直贏到季尾，依家主要一週一賽，大家有時間好好休養生息及有時間一齊操練。
球隊一直都係有質素，過去既事已經無辦法改變，唯有向前望繼續努力保持進步及爭取佳績，困難時期往往都會發生，過去三、四個星期的確好難挨，但一切已經過去，球隊需要用行動去回應，而對熱刺就係最好既例子！
完成非國盃歸隊後，我同高普講我希望立即為球隊上陣，我同佢講我準備就緒，我係職業球員，絕對唔會話攰或其他原因而唔上陣，對車路士及侯城我表現還可以，好開心能夠再次幫助球隊，但對熱刺明顯我覺得自己狀態更好，我希望黎緊繼續有好表現，並盡我所能踢好今季餘下每一場比賽。

James on a Lucas testimonial

James: Nothing official from the club as of yet. He certainly deserves one. It will be 10 years this summer since he signed and that’s some achievement when you consider he’s played under five different LFC managers. Lucas gets a lot of unfair stick. You don’t survive at a club as long as he has unless you are a top professional.

He could have left for Inter Milan in January but Klopp was glad he stayed put.
The club don’t have any set rules about testimonials. It’s not like if you are there for a decade you automatically get one but I hope they recognise Lucas’ service in that manner.

Hotel Principe Felipe in La Manga where #LFC will stay.

Dejan Lovren stayed behind as the Liverpool squad flew to Spain for their warm weather training camp.

Lovren is the only notable absentee with manager Jurgen Klopp including a number of Academy youngsters in his 25-man squad.

As expected the quartet of Danny Ings, Ovie Ejaria, Marko Grujic and Adam Bogdan didn't travel as they continue their rehabilitation.

Ings and Bogdan suffered season-ending knee injuries, while Ejaria ruptured his syndesmosis – the point just above the ankle where the tibia and the fibula bones meet – in last month's FA Cup defeat to Wolves.

Grujic hasn't featured since November. The young Serbian midfielder was on the bring of a comeback after an issue with his hamstring tendon when he suffered a setback in training.

Liverpool will arrive at La Manga Club in south east Spain on Wednesday evening. It will be their base for four nights. They fly home to Merseyside on Sunday.

http://www.liverpoolecho.co.uk/sport/football/football-news/lovren-left-behind-liverpool-jet-12610001

Pearce on loanees Flanagan & Ward

James: Yeah, we’re reaching that stage of the season where thoughts turn to whether those out on loan have a future at LFC.

In terms of Danny Ward, he’s rated very highly. Klopp wanted him to go to Huddersfield and play regularly at a decent level and he’s done that.

Whether Klopp sees him as a potential No 1 next season, I’m not sure. Time will tell.

As for Jon Flanagan, Klopp wanted him to get regular game time at Burnley. Unfortunately, that hasn’t worked out with Flanagan only making eight appearances for the Clarets so far. Hopefully he can enjoy a strong end to the season and give Klopp plenty to ponder.

But it’s clearly going to be tough because it’s not just Nathaniel Clyne he has to shift for that right-back spot. This season has also seen the emergence of teenager Trent Alexander-Arnold, who Klopp is a big fan of.

Pearce on what Klopp has planned in La Manga

James: Training, more training and some team bonding. He gave the players three days off after the win over Spurs and now it’s back down to business. He knows how valuable this time could prove to be.

Joel Matip opens up on the characters in the Anfield dressing room:
• Best friend?
I have no special mate but I think the language always helps.
Dejan (Lovren), Loris (Karius), Emre (Can), Ragnar (Klavan).
I always come and speak to them, I try to improve my language, it will be better if people understand me but sometimes I’m not so sure.”
• Funniest?
Dejan. He always has a funny joke or something like this, you can always laugh with him.
• Most talented?
Daniel (Sturridge) and Coutinho.
If you see them with the ball you don’t have to say something, everybody knows they’re amazing.
• Best hair?
I think Alberto (Moreno) speaks for itself. Always special. This one day he came with his blonde hair and I was like whoa.
• Worst hair?
It’s going to be hard. Also Alberto!
• Hardest player?
There’s a lot of hard players but also Dejan.
• The fittest?
Kev Stewart. I don’t know where he gets the power but he can run the whole time and I think he’ll never stop.
• Most likely to become TV pundit?
Henderson.
• Best passer?
Gini and Henderson.
• Best tackler?
Back to Dejan.
• Worst dresser?
Roberto Firmino and Alberto.
• Longest in front of the mirror?
I often see Loris in front of the mirror.

Jurgen Klopp conducted an FA Cup style draw to determine the sleeping arrangements on Liverpool's warm weather training camp in La Manga.

Players usually have their own rooms on away trips but Klopp decided he wanted them to share during their four-night stay at the five-star Hotel Principe Felipe as part of his team building exercise.

Reds captain Jordan Henderson is in with Sheyi Ojo, while vice-captain James Milner has Sadio Mane for company.

“We had this little funny gimmick. Bringing the players together by putting them in twin rooms,” Klopp told the ECHO.

“It wasn't a free choice! I picked the names out of the hat.

“Lallana and Manninger. Henderson is with Ojo, Milner with Sadio. Firmino and Karius – nice room!

“Simon Mignolet is with Conor Masterson, I'm not sure that Simon even knew Conor before this trip. But after four days here he really will.

“The players are learning more about each other. Everything has been perfectly organised.

“We've had a few meetings and we'll have a few more of them. I'm enjoying it and I think the players are too.”

Five starts says it all. But it's not just the England man who has been struggling for action in the top flight.

It's even worse for Alberto Moreno, who has began only two league games compared to 28 the previous year.

All of this points to one undeniable truth. Beyond the first 13 or 14 players in his squad, Klopp doesn't trust – or rate – most of the alternatives at present.
